title: The Library of Shapes
summary: How adaptive immunity remembers — B-cells, antibodies, and the body's quiet library of microbe shapes. Trauma-informed register: no warfare vocabulary; the immune system learns and remembers rather than 'fights'.

questions.prompt: Adaptive immunity is different from innate immunity because it…
choices: Learns the shape of each microbe and remembers it next time
Works faster than innate immunity on the first encounter
Only protects the skin
Replaces innate immunity once you're older
questions.explanation: Adaptive immunity is slower the first time you meet a microbe — but it remembers, so the next encounter is faster and calmer.

questions.prompt: A B-cell's main job is to…
choices: Make antibodies — small proteins shaped to match a specific microbe
Carry oxygen through the blood
Send signals to the stomach
Store calcium for bones
questions.explanation: B-cells produce antibodies. Each B-cell makes antibodies shaped to recognize one specific kind of microbe.

questions.prompt: An antibody recognizes a microbe by…
choices: Matching its shape to a matching shape on the microbe's surface
Listening for sounds the microbe makes
Reading the microbe's DNA letter by letter
Smelling the microbe through the bloodstream
questions.explanation: Antibodies are shaped to fit specific molecules on a microbe's surface — a little like a key fitting a lock.

questions.prompt: Memory B-cells help your body the next time you meet the same microbe by…
choices: Recognizing the shape quickly and making the right antibodies sooner
Asking your brain to remember
Producing red blood cells faster
Raising your temperature for the rest of your life
questions.explanation: Memory B-cells are why your second meeting with a microbe is usually shorter and gentler than the first.

questions.prompt: Vaccines work with your adaptive immunity by…
choices: Showing the body the shape of a microbe so memory B-cells form ahead of time
Adding more red blood cells
Replacing your immune system entirely
Removing all microbes from your body
questions.explanation: A vaccine introduces the shape of a microbe — or part of it — so the body can practice. If the real microbe shows up later, memory B-cells are already ready.

questions.prompt: Most microbes the adaptive immune system meets are…
choices: Quiet helpers it learns to recognize and leave alone
Always dangerous
Too small to be recognized
Made of metal
questions.explanation: Adaptive immunity learns which microbes are part of normal life and which are not. Most of what it meets is friendly — it remembers that too.
